title: Delete primary only
description: Deletion removes the main row but leaves caches, indexes, analytics, exports, embeddings, or external copies.
body: Failure mode: account deletion or retention cleanup targets one table while derived stores continue to expose personal data. Detection signals include separate search indexes, denormalized profile fields, event streams, files, embeddings, and support exports with no cleanup path. Remediation is propagation, disassociation, or documented manual handling. Human gate is required when regulated data or user-rights requests cannot be fully handled. Use when a proposed approach skips personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion.
